# PyHPS

{{ pyansys }} {{ python }} {{ pypi }} {{ GH-CI }} {{ codecov }} {{ MIT }} {{ black }} {{ pre-commit }}

PyHPS is a Python client library for Ansys HPC Platform Services (HPS), which is
a set of technology components designed to help you manage the execution of simulations
while making use of your full range of computing assets.

PyHPS brings Ansys HPS to your Python app. Wrapping around Ansys HPS REST APIs, PyHPS
allows you to:

- Create projects and modify existing ones.
- Monitor and manage jobs.
- Run your own design exploration algorithms.
- Retrieve simulation results.

## Documentation and issues

Documentation for the latest stable release of PyHPS is hosted at
[PyHPS documentation](https://hps.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/).

In the upper right corner of the documentation's title bar, there is an option
for switching from viewing the documentation for the latest stable release
to viewing the documentation for the development version or previously
released versions.

The PyHPS documentation contains these sections:

- [Getting started](https://hps.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/getting_started/index.html): Explains
  how to install PyHPS in user mode.
- [User guide](https://hps.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/user_guide/index.html): Describes the basics
  of how to use PyHPS to interact with Ansys HPS.
- [Examples](https://hps.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/examples/index.html): Provides examples of how
  to interact with Ansys HPS in Python using PyHPS.
- [API reference](https://hps.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/api/index.html): Describes PyHPS functions,
  classes, methods, and their parameters and return values so that you can understand how to
  interact with them programmatically
- [Contribute](https://hps.docs.pyansys.com/version/dev/contribute.html): Provides information on
  how to install PyHPS in developer mode and make contributions to the codebase and documentation.

On the [PyHPS Issues](https://github.com/ansys/pyhps/issues) page, you can
create issues to report bugs and request new features. On the
[PyHPS Discussions](https://github.com/ansys/pyhps/discussions) page or the
[Discussions](https://discuss.ansys.com/) page on the Ansys Developer portal,
you can post questions, share ideas, and get community feedback.

To reach the project support team, email [pyansys.core@ansys.com](mailto:pyansys.core@ansys.com).

## License

PyHPS is licensed under the MIT license.

PyHPS makes no commercial claim over Ansys whatsoever. This library extends the
functionality of Ansys HPC Platform Services by adding a Python interface to it
without changing the core behavior or license of the original software. The use
of PyHPS requires a legally licensed local copy of Ansys HPC Platform Services.

To get a copy of Ansys HPC Platform Services, see the [Ansys HPC Platform Services Guide](https://ansyshelp.ansys.com/account/secured?returnurl=/Views/Secured/hpcplat/v000/en/rep_ug/rep_ug.html)
in the Ansys Help.

classes.name: ansys.hps.client.exceptions.HPSError
documentation:
Provides the base class for all HPS-related errors.

This class derives from the {class}`requests.exceptions.RequestException`
base class.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> from ansys.hps.client import HPSError
  >>> from ansys.hps.client.jms import Client
  >>> try:
  >>>     client = Client(url="https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps/",
                          username="repuser",
                          password="wrong_psw")
  >>> except HPSError as e:
  >>>     print(e)
  401 Client Error: invalid_grant for: POST https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps/auth...
  Invalid user credentials
  ```

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.client.Client.refresh_access_token
documentation: Request a new access token

Provides the Python client to the HPS APIs.

This class uses the provided credentials to create and store
an authorized {class}`requests.Session` object.

The following alternative authentication workflows are supported
and evaluated in the order listed:

- Access token: No authentication is needed.
- Username and password: The client connects to the OAuth server and
  requests access and refresh tokens.
- Refresh token: The client connects to the OAuth server and
  requests a new access token.
- Client credentials: The client authenticates with the `client_id` and `client_secret`
  parameters to obtain a new access token. (A refresh token is not included.)

# Parameters

url

: Base path for the server to call. The default is `'https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps'`.

username

: Username.

password

: Password.

realm

: Keycloak realm. The default is `'rep'`.

grant_type

: Authentication method. The default is `'password'`.

scope

: String containing one or more requested scopes. The default is `'openid'`.

client_id

: Client type. The default is `'rep-cli'`.

client_secret

: Client secret. The default is `None`.

access_token

: Access token.

refresh_token

: Refresh token.

auth_url : str, optional
all_fields : bool, optional

> Whether to apply the `fields="all"` query parameter to all requests so
> that all available fields are returned for the requested resources. The
> default is `True`.

verify

: If a Boolean, whether to verify the server's TLS certificate. The default
  is None\`, which disables certificate validation and warns the user about it.
  If a string, the path to the CA bundle to use. For more information,
  see the {class}`requests.Session` documentation.

disable_security_warnings

: Whether to disable urllib3 warnings about insecure HTTPS requests. The default is `True`.
  For more information, see urllib3 documentation about TLS warnings.

# Examples

Create a client object and connect to HPS with a username and password.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.hps.client import Client
>>> cl = Client(
...     url="https://localhost:8443/hps",
...     username="repuser",
...     password="repuser"
... )
```

Create a client object and connect to HPS with a refresh token.

```pycon
>>> cl = Client(
...     url="https://localhost:8443/hps",
...     username="repuser",
...     refresh_token="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cC..."
>>> )
```

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.auth.api.auth_api.AuthApi.get_users
documentation:
Get users, filtered according to query parameters.

Examples of query parameters are:

- `username`
- `firstName`
- `lastName`
- `exact`

Pagination is also supported using the `first` and `max` parameters.

For a list of supported query parameters, see the
[Keycloak API documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/documentation).

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.auth.api.auth_api.AuthApi.get_user
documentation: Get the user representation for a given user ID.

documentation: Get the name of the groups that the user belongs to.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.auth.api.auth_api.AuthApi.get_user_realm_roles_names
documentation: Get the name of the realm roles for the user.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.auth.api.auth_api.AuthApi.get_user_groups
documentation: Get the groups that the user belongs to.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.auth.api.auth_api.AuthApi.get_user_realm_roles
documentation: Get the realm roles for the user.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.auth.api.auth_api.AuthApi.user_is_admin
documentation: Determine if the user is a system administrator.

Provides a minimal wrapper around the Keycloak API to query user information.

# Parameters

client

: HPS client object.

# Examples

Get users whose first name contains `john`.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.hps.client import Client
>>> from ansys.hps.client.auth import AuthApi, User
>>> cl = Client(
...     url="https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps", username="repuser", password="repuser"
... )
>>> auth_api = AuthApi(cl)
>>> users = auth_api.get_users(firstName="john", exact=False)
```

functions.name: ansys.hps.client.authenticate.authenticate
documentation:
Authenticates the user with a password or refresh token against the HPS authentication service.

If this method is successful, the response includes access and refresh tokens.

# Parameters

url

: Base path for the server to call. The default is `'https://127.0.0.1:8443/rep'`.

realm

: Keycloak realm. The default is `'rep'`.

grant_type: str, optional

: Authentication method. The default is `'password'`.

scope

: String containing one or more requested scopes. The default is `'openid'`.

client_id

: Client type. The default is `'rep-cli'`.

client_secret

: Client secret. The default is `None`.

username

: Username.

password

: Password.

refresh_token

: Refresh token.

timeout

: Timeout in seconds. The default is `10.0`.

verify: Union[bool, str], optional

: If a Boolean, whether to verify the server's TLS certificate. If a string, the
  path to the CA bundle to use. For more information, see the {class}`requests.Session`
  documentation.

# Returns

dict

: JSON-encoded content of a {class}`requests.Response` object.

classes.name: ansys.hps.client.jms.resource.file.File
documentation:
Provides the file resource.

# Parameters

src

: Client-only field to specify either the path of an input file
  or a file-like object. In the latter case, `requests` recommends that
  you open files in binary mode.

id

: Unique ID to access the resource, generated internally by the server on creation.

name

: Name of the file resource.

type

: Type of the file. While you can use any string, you should use the correct media type
  for the given resource.

storage_id

: ID of the file in the (orthogonal) file storage system.

size : int, optional
hash : str, optional
creation_time : datetime, optional

> Date and time that the file resource was created.

modification_time

: Date and time that the file resource was last modified.

created_by

: ID of the user who created the object.

modified_by

: ID of the user who last modified the object.

format : str, optional
expiry_time : datetime, optional

> File expiration time.

evaluation_path

: Relative path for the file instance to store the job evaluation under.

monitor

: Whether to live monitor the file's content.

collect

: Whether the file should be collected per job.

collect_interval

: Collection frequency for a file with `collect=True`. The minimum value
  is limited by the evaluator's settings. A value of `0` or `None` indicates
  that the evaluator should decide. Another value indicates the interval in seconds.

reference_id

: ID of the reference file that this file was created from.

classes.name: ansys.hps.client.jms.resource.fitness_definition.FitnessTermDefinition
documentation:
Provides the fitness term definition resource.

# Parameters

id

: Unique ID to access the resource, generated internally by the server on creation.

name

: Name of the fitness term.

expression

: Python expression that defines the fitness term.

type

: Fitness term type.

weighting_factor

: Relative importance of the fitness term in comparison to other fitness terms.

# Examples

Create a fitness term of the objective type.

```pycon
>>> ft1 = FitnessTermDefinition(
...     name="weight",
...     type="design_objective",
...     weighting_factor=1.0,
...     expression="map_design_objective(values['weight'],7.5,5.5)"
... )
```

Create a fitness term of the target constraint type.

```pycon
>>> ft2 = FitnessTermDefinition(
...     name="torsional_stiffness",
...     type="target_constraint",
...     weighting_factor=0.8,
...     expression="map_target_constraint(
...         values['torsion_stiffness'], 1313.0, 5.0, 30.0)"
... )
```

Create a fitness term of the limit constraint type.

```pycon
>>> ft3 = FitnessTermDefinition(
...     name="max_stress",
...     type="limit_constraint",
...     weighting_factor=0.6,
...     expression="map_limit_constraint(
...         values['max_stress'], 451.0, 50.0 )"
... )
```

>>> fd.add_fitness_term(name="torsional_stiffness",

```

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.jms.api.jms_api.JmsApi.get_project_by_name
documentation:
Query projects by name.

If no projects are found, an empty list is returned.

In multiple projects with same name are found, what is returned depends
on the `last_created` value:

- If `last_created=True`, the last created project is returned.
- If `last_created=False`, the full list of projects with the given name is returned.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.jms.api.jms_api.JmsApi.copy_task_definition_templates
documentation:
Create task definition templates by copying existing templates.

# Parameters

templates

: List of task definition template. Note that only the `id` field of
  `TaskDefinitionTemplate` objects must be filled. The other fields can be empty.

wait

: Whether to wait for the copy to complete. The default is `True`.

# Returns

Union\[List[str], str\]

: If `wait=True`, returns the list of newly created template IDs.
  If `wait=False`, returns an operation ID that can be used to
  track progress.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.jms.api.jms_api.JmsApi.monitor_operation
documentation:
Poll an operation until it is completed using an exponential backoff.

Parameters
----------
operation_id : str
    ID of the operation to monitor.
max_value: float, optional
    Maximum interval in seconds between consecutive calls.
max_time: float, optional
    Maximum time in seconds to pool the operation before giving up.

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.jms.api.jms_api.JmsApi.get_storage
documentation: Get a list of storages.
Storages

Wraps around the JMS root endpoints.

# Parameters

client

: HPS client object.

# Examples

Create a project.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.hps.client import Client
>>> from ansys.hps.client.jms import JmsApi, Project
>>> cl = Client(
...     url="https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps", username="repuser", password="repuser"
... )
>>> jms_api = JmsApi(cl)
>>> project = jms_api.create_project(Project(name="Example project"))
```

documentation: Delete objects.

Exposes the project endpoints of the JMS.

# Parameters

client

: HPS client object.

project_id

: ID of the project.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.hps.client import Client
>>> from ansys.hps.client.jms import JmsApi, Project, ProjectApi
>>> cl = Client(
...     url="https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps", username="repuser", password="repuser"
... )
>>> project = Project(name="Example project")
>>> print(project)
{
    "name": "Example project"
}
>>> jms_api = JmsApi(cl)
>>> project = jms_api.create_project(project)
>>> print(project)
{
    "id": "02qtyJfpfAQ0fr3zkoIAfC",
    "name": "Example project",
    "active": false,
    "priority": 1,
    "creation_time": ...
    ...
}
>>> project_api = ProjectApi(cl, project.id)
>>> print(project_api)
'https://127.0.0.1:8443/hps/jms/api/v1/projects/02qtyJfpfAQ0fr3zkoIAfC'
>>> jobs = project_api.get_jobs()
```

methods.name: ansys.hps.client.rms.api.rms_api.RmsApi.update_evaluator_configuration
documentation:
Update an evaluator configuration.

# Examples

Set a custom resource property on a Linux evaluator that was active in the past 60 seconds.

```pycon
>>> import datetime
>>> from ansys.hps.client import Client
>>> from ansys.hps.client.jms import RmsApi, EvaluatorConfigurationUpdate
>>> cl = Client(
...     url="https://localhost:8443/hps", username="repuser", password="repuser"
... )
>>> rms_api = RmsApi(cl)
>>> query_params = {
...     "platform" : "linux",
...     "update_time.gt" : datetime.datetime.utcnow() - datetime.timedelta(seconds=60)
... }
>>> evaluator = rms_api.get_evaluators(fields=["id", "host_id"], **query_params)[0]
>>> config_update = EvaluatorConfigurationUpdate(
...    custom_resource_properties={"disk_type" : "SSD"}
... )
>>> rms_api.update_evaluator_configuration(ev.id, config_update)
```
